ucs.title: [LLM] Aoqin Dragon USB-borne fake-document executable launch (Mongall/Heyoka dropper)
ucs.description: Hunts Aoqin Dragon's signature USB-worm initial access: an executable launched from removable media (drive letters D:–Z:) whose filename masquerades as a document, folder, or recycle bin (double-extension `.doc.exe`/`.pdf.exe`, or names like `My Document.exe`, `Recycle.exe`, `System Volume.exe`). This is the dropper pattern observed across 10 years of Aoqin Dragon campaigns delivering the Mongall and Heyoka backdoors to Australian, Cambodian, Hong Kong, Singaporean and Vietnamese government / education / telco targets.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="*.exe" Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where match(process_path,"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\\\") | eval doubleExt=if(match(process_name,"(?i)\.(doc|docx|pdf|xls|xlsx|ppt|pptx|rtf|jpg|png)\.exe$"),1,0), lureName=if(match(process_name,"(?i)(recycle|my document|system volume|autorun|removable|new folder)\.exe$"),1,0), recycleCmd=if(match(cmdline,"(?i)\$RECYCLE\.BIN|RECYCLER"),1,0) | where doubleExt=1 OR lureName=1 OR recycleCmd=1 | join type=left dest [| tstats summariesonly=false values(Filesystem.file_name) as droppedFiles values(Filesystem.file_path) as droppedPaths from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action="created" by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent_process_name cmdline droppedFiles | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let _lure_names = dynamic(["\\Recycle.exe","\\My Document.exe","\\System Volume.exe","\\autorun.exe","\\New Folder.exe","\\Removable Disk.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr"
// Removable-media drive letters (Defender records full paths in FolderPath)
| where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
// Aoqin Dragon's masquerading: double-extension OR document/recycle lure name
| where FileName matches regex @"(?i)\.(doc|docx|pdf|xls|xlsx|ppt|pptx|rtf|jpg|png)\.exe$"
   or FolderPath has_any (_lure_names)
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\$RECYCLE\.BIN|RECYCLER\\"
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FolderPath matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
    | summarize DroppedFiles = make_set(FileName, 25), DroppedHashes = make_set(SHA256, 25) by DeviceId
  ) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LureBinary = FileName, LurePath = FolderPath, SHA256,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          DroppedFiles, DroppedHashes
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _lure_names = dynamic(["\\Recycle.exe","\\My Document.exe","\\System Volume.exe","\\autorun.exe","\\New Folder.exe","\\Removable Disk.exe"]);
ImProcessCreate
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
| where TargetProcessName endswith ".exe" or TargetProcessName endswith ".scr"
| where TargetProcessName matches regex @"(?i)^[D-Z]:\\"
| where TargetProcessName matches regex @"(?i)\.(doc|docx|pdf|xls|xlsx|ppt|pptx|rtf|jpg|png)\.exe$"
   or TargetProcessName has_any (_lure_names)
   or TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)\$RECYCLE\.BIN|RECYCLER\\"
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          LureBinary = TargetProcessName,
          CommandLine = TargetProcessCommandLine,
          Parent = ParentProcessName,
          ParentCmd = ParentProcessCommandLine,
          Hash = SHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Aoqin Dragon USB-Borne Fake-Document Executable
id: 7c1f4d32-9a2b-4e8a-9f4b-aoqindragon01
status: experimental
description: Detects an executable launched from removable media (D:-Z:) whose filename masquerades as a document, folder, or recycle-bin item. Signature USB-worm dropper pattern of Aoqin Dragon (G1007 / UNC94) used to deploy the Mongall and Heyoka backdoors against APAC government, education, and telecom targets since 2013.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1007/
  - https://www.sentinelone.com/labs/aoqin-dragon-newly-discovered-chinese-linked-apt-has-been-quietly-spying-on-organizations-for-10-years/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1091
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.t1036.007
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.g1007
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_removable_path:
    Image|re: '(?i)^[D-Z]:\\'
  selection_lure_double_ext:
    Image|re: '(?i)\.(doc|docx|pdf|xls|xlsx|ppt|pptx|rtf|jpg|png)\.exe$'
  selection_lure_name:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\Recycle.exe'
      - '\My Document.exe'
      - '\System Volume.exe'
      - '\autorun.exe'
      - '\New Folder.exe'
      - '\Removable Disk.exe'
  selection_recycle_cmd:
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)\$RECYCLE\.BIN|RECYCLER\\'
  condition: selection_removable_path and (selection_lure_double_ext or selection_lure_name or selection_recycle_cmd)
falsepositives:
  - Portable applications shipped on USB drives by IT (rare, baseline these)
  - Vendor diagnostic tools auto-launched from optical media
level: high
ucs.rationale: This pattern is uniquely Aoqin Dragon. The SentinelOne 2022 disclosure (and the MITRE G1007 entry) document that the group's defining capability is a USB worm that drops executables onto removable media disguised with document and folder icons (T1036) — names like 'My Document.exe', 'Recycle.exe', 'doc.exe', and the double-extension pattern observed across their Mongall and Heyoka campaigns. Few l
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] Aoqin Dragon Heyoka backdoor DNS-tunneling C2
ucs.description: Hunts the DNS-tunnel C2 channel of the Heyoka backdoor — an open-source DNS-tunneling proof-of-concept that Aoqin Dragon weaponised and deployed alongside Mongall. Looks for high-cardinality long-label DNS queries (TXT/A/AAAA) issued from non-resolver processes to a single apex domain, the network fingerprint of Heyoka's spoofed-source DNS exfiltration.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=false count from datamodel=Network_Resolution where DNS.message_type="QUERY" DNS.query_type IN ("TXT","A","AAAA") by DNS.src DNS.query DNS.query_type _time span=1h | `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)` | rex field=query "^(?<first_label>[^\.]+)\." | rex field=query "(?<apex>[^\.]+\.[^\.]+)$" | eval label_len=len(first_label) | where label_len > 30 | stats count as queries dcount(query) as unique_labels values(query_type) as qtypes max(label_len) as max_label_len by src apex | where unique_labels > 50 AND queries > 100 | sort - unique_labels
ucs.kql:
let _resolver_procs = dynamic(["svchost.exe","chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","brave.exe","outlook.exe","teams.exe","onedrive.exe"]);
DeviceEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse"
| extend QName = tolower(tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName))
| where isnotempty(QName)
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(QName, ".")[0])
| extend LabelCount = array_length(split(QName, "."))
| where LabelCount >= 3
| extend Apex = strcat(tostring(split(QName, ".")[LabelCount-2]), ".", tostring(split(QName, ".")[LabelCount-1]))
// Heyoka encodes payload bytes into long alphanumeric subdomain labels
| where strlen(FirstLabel) > 30
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (_resolver_procs)
| summarize Queries = count(),
            UniqueLabels = dcount(QName),
            MaxLabelLen = max(strlen(FirstLabel)),
            SampleQuery = any(QName)
            by DeviceId, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessSHA256, Apex, bin(Timestamp, 1h)
| where UniqueLabels > 50 and Queries > 100
// Suppress legitimate high-cardinality apexes (CDN, AV cloud-lookup)
| where Apex !endswith "akamai.net" and Apex !endswith "cloudfront.net"
     and Apex !endswith "trendmicro.com" and Apex !endswith "mcafee.com"
     and Apex !endswith "microsoft.com" and Apex !endswith "windows.com"
     and Apex !endswith "office.com" and Apex !endswith "office365.com"
| order by UniqueLabels desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _resolver_hosts = dynamic(["akamai.net","cloudfront.net","trendmicro.com","mcafee.com","microsoft.com","windows.com","office.com","office365.com","googleusercontent.com"]);
DnsEvents
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where QueryType in ("TXT","A","AAAA","1","16","28")
| where isnotempty(Name)
| extend QName = tolower(Name)
| extend FirstLabel = tostring(split(QName, ".")[0])
| extend LabelCount = array_length(split(QName, "."))
| where LabelCount >= 3
| extend Apex = strcat(tostring(split(QName, ".")[LabelCount-2]), ".", tostring(split(QName, ".")[LabelCount-1]))
| where strlen(FirstLabel) > 30
| where not(Apex has_any (_resolver_hosts))
| summarize Queries = count(),
            UniqueLabels = dcount(QName),
            MaxLabelLen = max(strlen(FirstLabel)),
            QueryTypes = make_set(QueryType, 5),
            SampleQuery = any(QName)
            by ClientIP, Computer, Apex, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where UniqueLabels > 50 and Queries > 100
| order by UniqueLabels desc
ucs.rationale: Aoqin Dragon's documented backdoor toolkit centres on Heyoka, a public DNS-tunneling PoC the group customised (T1587.001 in the actor's MITRE profile). Heyoka's distinguishing feature is encoding C2 traffic into long base32-style subdomain labels under an attacker-controlled apex, producing the high-unique-label / long-first-label / non-resolver-process fingerprint this hunt anchors on. Generic 'D
